setup.title: Improve your muscle memory by practicing writing
setup.copy: Choose Kana or Kanji options, grab a pen and paper, and click **Start**.
setup.copy2: Write the Kana or Kanji before time runs out, then watch the stroke animations.
script.katakanaDescription: Used for words of foreign origin
about.title: About
about.whyCopy: In the process of learning Japanese I realised that recognising and reading the characters was not enough. When I had to write them, my mind went blank because I had not built the muscle memory to write them quickly.
about.whyCopy2: Most flashcard apps show hiragana or katakana and ask you to read it. This game reverses that process: it gives you the reading and asks you to write the character on paper before revealing the stroke animations.
about.source: You can see this project's source code at
about.kanjiCredit: Kanji stroke-animation SVG files were copied from
about.kanaCredit: Hiragana and katakana stroke-animation SVG files were copied from
about.referencesCopy: Although I did not copy content directly from the following projects, I used them as references and as secondary sources when cross-checking kanji information. They are also valuable resources for further study.
about.copyrightCopy: This site is an open-source project maintained under the GNU AGPL license. Feel free to fork, contribute, or share it with other learners.

finished.title: Round complete
finished.copy: The current pool is empty. Restart with the same selection or adjust it first.
